refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall provide an integrated performance requirement derivation, recording, and management system that translates worksite mission context (site conditions, operational policies, safety constraints) into documented performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) with full traceability to source inputs and maintained history of baseline calibrations and updates.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks specificity on the derivation mechanism, traceability structure, and historical management. Refinement clarifies that the system must bridge worksite context to quantified performance targets, maintain audit trails, and support iterative calibration—critical for safety-critical systems and stakeholder confidence (Site Manager, Safety Manager, Maintenance/Quality).
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (1) performance targets are derived from documented worksite inputs via a defined algorithm or decision logic; (2) traceability matrix links each target to source constraints; (3) baseline history is recorded with timestamps and change justification; (4) acceptance criteria are met per system specification.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The system shall accept worksite mission context inputs (site conditions, operational policies, safety plan constraints) in a structured format.
CR-NGHE-005.2: The system shall apply a derivation algorithm to translate mission context into quantified performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins).
CR-NGHE-005.3: The system shall maintain a traceability matrix linking each performance target to its source worksite input and derivation rationale.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The system shall record and version-control baseline performance targets with timestamps, change logs, and calibration update history.
CR-NGHE-005.5: The system shall provide query and reporting interfaces to retrieve performance targets and historical baselines for stakeholder review and audit.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized performance management database with rule-based derivation engine that ingests worksite context via API, applies configurable performance calculation rules, and maintains versioned baseline records with full audit trail.

DP-B: Distributed performance derivation across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, with each module computing local performance targets and synchronizing via a shared registry; central Safety Services module validates and archives.
DP-C: Manual performance target specification by Site Manager via a web portal, with system recording inputs and maintaining change history; no automated derivation logic.

DP-D: Hybrid approach: automated derivation engine in a dedicated Performance Management module that consumes worksite context from Safety Services and Vehicle telemetry, produces targets, and exposes targets via Telemetry interface for consumption by Vehicle and Teleoperation.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Hybrid approach with a dedicated Performance Management logical component that integrates worksite context (from Safety Services and Vehicle operational state), applies a configurable derivation algorithm, records versioned baselines, and exposes performance targets via standardized Telemetry interface for consumption by Vehicle and Teleoperation modules. This design balances automation (DP-A strength) with distributed awareness (DP-B strength) while maintaining a single source of truth for performance targets.

potential_sources: Dependency on Safety Services to provide worksite context and safety constraints (acceptable; Safety Services is a known module).
Dependency on Vehicle telemetry for operational state inputs (acceptable; Telemetry is a known interface).
Exposure of performance targets via Telemetry interface (acceptable; Telemetry is a known interface).
No circular dependencies detected; information flow is acyclic (worksite context → derivation → targets → consumption).
coupling_check.notes: DP-D exhibits low coupling to specific Vehicle or Teleoperation implementations; it depends on stable interfaces (Telemetry, Safety context) rather than internal module logic. This supports modularity and future evolution.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite context can be reliably captured and provided by Safety Services in a structured format. Risk: If Safety Services does not expose context in a machine-readable form, derivation engine cannot function. Mitigation: Define Safety Services interface contract early.

Assumption: Performance derivation algorithm is deterministic and can be expressed as configurable rules. Risk: If derivation requires subjective judgment or real-time optimization, a rule-based approach may be insufficient. Mitigation: Prototype derivation logic with Site Manager and Safety Manager early.

Assumption: Vehicle and Teleoperation modules can consume performance targets via Telemetry interface without modification. Risk: If modules expect targets in a different format or update frequency, integration friction may arise. Mitigation: Define Telemetry schema for performance targets in interface specification.

Assumption: Historical baseline data volume is manageable (e.g., < 1 GB per year). Risk: If calibration updates occur at high frequency, storage and query performance may degrade. Mitigation: Define retention policy and archival strategy.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-D is sound. Recommend: (1) Explicitly define the derivation algorithm (rule set, decision tree, or optimization model) in the design specification; (2) Specify the Telemetry interface schema for performance targets (JSON, protobuf, or other); (3) Define the Safety Services interface contract for worksite context inputs; (4) Establish baseline versioning and change control procedures.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary. DP-D (Performance Management logical component) shall be realized as a subsystem within NGHE Solution. This assignment reflects that performance requirement derivation and management is a system-level concern that integrates inputs from multiple lower-level modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and serves the overall mission.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Inbound: Vehicle operational state (speed, fuel consumption, cycle time, availability metrics) and worksite context (site conditions, operational policies, safety constraints) are received via Telemetry. Outbound: Derived performance targets (cycle time target, energy efficiency target, availability target, safety margins) are published via Telemetry for consumption by Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Inbound: Safety constraints and worksite safety plan updates are received via SafetyAlert interface from Safety Services module. These inputs inform performance target derivation (e.g., safety margins, availability requirements).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Inbound: Mission context and operational directives from Site Manager or Tele-operator are received via Tasking interface. These inputs provide worksite mission parameters (e.g., production targets, site conditions) that drive performance requirement derivation.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level boundary. Performance requirement derivation and management is a core system function that integrates inputs from multiple subsystems and serves the overall mission. Containment is high because the function is system-level and not specific to any single subsystem (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Diffusion risk is minimal; the function is cohesive and does not naturally decompose across multiple modules.

Vehicle module is responsible for executing tasks and reporting operational state. Performance requirement derivation is a system-level concern, not a vehicle-specific function. Assigning DP-D to Vehicle would create tight coupling between vehicle control logic and system-level performance management, violating separation of concerns. Diffusion risk is high; the function would be isolated from other system inputs (Safety Services, Teleoperation context).

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operation and monitoring. Performance requirement derivation is not a teleoperation-specific function. Assigning DP-D to Teleoperation would create asymmetry (autonomous Vehicle would not have access to performance targets) and violate system-level integration. Diffusion risk is high.

Safety Services module provides safety constraints and worksite context. While Safety Services is a source of inputs to performance derivation, the derivation logic itself is not a safety-specific function. Assigning DP-D to Safety Services would conflate safety constraint management with performance target derivation, reducing modularity. Diffusion risk is moderate; the function would be separated from Vehicle operational state inputs (Telemetry).

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Performance requirement derivation and management is a system-level integrating function that consumes inputs from multiple subsystems (Vehicle, Safety Services, Teleoperation) and produces outputs consumed by multiple subsystems. It is best contained within the NGHE Solution boundary as a dedicated logical component (e.g., Performance Management subsystem) that operates at the system level and maintains a single source of truth for performance targets. This design ensures cohesion, minimizes diffusion, and supports traceability and audit requirements.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution is not decomposed into subsystems, the Performance Management component may become a monolithic black box, reducing visibility and testability. Mitigation: Define Performance Management as a distinct subsystem within NGHE Solution with clear interfaces and internal structure.

Risk: If the Telemetry interface is not well-specified, Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may not correctly consume performance targets. Mitigation: Define Telemetry schema and versioning strategy early; include performance target schema in interface specification.

Risk: If Safety Services does not expose worksite context in a machine-readable format, the Performance Management component cannot automate derivation. Mitigation: Establish interface contract with Safety Services; define worksite context data model.

Risk: If performance derivation algorithm is complex or requires frequent updates, the NGHE Solution module may become a bottleneck for system evolution. Mitigation: Design derivation algorithm as a configurable rule engine; support dynamic rule updates without code changes.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assignment of DP-D to NGHE Solution is sound. Recommend: (1) Define Performance Management as a distinct subsystem within NGHE Solution with clear responsibilities and interfaces; (2) Specify Telemetry interface schema for performance targets (include fields for cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins, derivation timestamp, baseline version); (3) Establish interface contracts with Safety Services (worksite context inputs) and Vehicle/Teleoperation (performance target consumption); (4) Design derivation algorithm as a configurable rule engine to support future evolution; (5) Implement baseline versioning and change control procedures in Performance Management subsystem.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (hybrid performance management with dedicated derivation engine) directly addresses CR-NGHE-005 Refined. The DP specifies how to derive performance targets from worksite context (via configurable algorithm), record them (via versioned baseline storage), and manage them (via Telemetry interface exposure). The DP is solution-neutral (does not prescribe implementation technology) and supports all sub-FRs (context input, derivation, traceability, versioning, reporting). No FR-DP mismatch detected.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk (Low): Performance Management component is assigned to NGHE Solution (system-level module). This is appropriate because the function integrates inputs from multiple subsystems and serves the overall mission. No diffusion across Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services modules is expected. However, if NGHE Solution is not further decomposed, the component may become monolithic. Mitigation: Define Performance Management as a distinct subsystem with clear interfaces.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment score for NGHE Solution is 95/100, indicating strong alignment. The function is system-level and integrating, not specific to any single subsystem. Assignment to NGHE Solution maintains separation of concerns and supports modularity. External interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking) are all known and stable, reducing integration risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-005 Refined is feasible. Acceptance criteria (documented performance targets, traceability, baseline history) can be verified via: (1) inspection of performance target records and traceability matrix; (2) review of baseline version history and change logs; (3) testing of derivation algorithm against worksite context inputs; (4) audit of Telemetry interface for performance target publication. Verification does not require specialized test equipment; it is primarily a data and process verification activity.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of CR-NGHE-005 to NGHE Solution is clear. The refined FR specifies what the system must do (derive, record, manage performance targets). The assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is the system-level boundary responsible for integrating subsystems and delivering system-level functions. No ambiguity in allocation; ownership is clear.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Define Performance Management as a distinct subsystem within NGHE Solution in the system architecture diagram. This will improve visibility and support future decomposition.
Revision 2: Specify the derivation algorithm (rule set, decision tree, or optimization model) in the design specification. Include examples of how worksite context inputs map to performance targets.

Revision 3: Define the Telemetry interface schema for performance targets. Include fields for cycle time target, energy efficiency target, availability target, safety margins, derivation timestamp, baseline version, and traceability reference.

Revision 4: Establish interface contracts with Safety Services (worksite context inputs) and Vehicle/Teleoperation (performance target consumption). Define data models, update frequencies, and error handling.
Revision 5: Define baseline versioning and change control procedures. Specify how baseline updates are triggered, approved, and recorded in the history.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 Refined is not atomic; it decomposes into five sub-FRs (context input, derivation, traceability, versioning, reporting). Each sub-FR requires further design and allocation to ensure completeness and traceability. The refined FR and selected DP are sound and pass all gates, but the next level of decomposition is necessary to detail the derivation algorithm, interface specifications, and implementation approach. Proceeding to the next level will clarify design decisions and support detailed design and implementation.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The system shall accept worksite mission context inputs (site conditions, operational policies, safety plan constraints) in a structured format.
CR-NGHE-005.2: The system shall apply a derivation algorithm to translate mission context into quantified performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins).
CR-NGHE-005.3: The system shall maintain a traceability matrix linking each performance target to its source worksite input and derivation rationale.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The system shall record and version-control baseline performance targets with timestamps, change logs, and calibration update history.
CR-NGHE-005.5: The system shall provide query and reporting interfaces to retrieve performance targets and historical baselines for stakeholder review and audit.

assumptions: Worksite context can be reliably captured and provided by Safety Services in a structured format.
Performance derivation algorithm is deterministic and can be expressed as configurable rules.
Vehicle and Teleoperation modules can consume performance targets via Telemetry interface without modification.
Historical baseline data volume is manageable (< 1 GB per year).
Site Manager, Safety Manager, and Maintenance/Quality stakeholders will provide input to derivation algorithm design and validation.
NGHE Solution will be decomposed into subsystems, including a dedicated Performance Management subsystem.
open_risks: Risk: Derivation algorithm complexity may exceed rule-based approach capability. Mitigation: Prototype with stakeholders early; consider machine learning or optimization models if needed.
Risk: Safety Services interface may not expose worksite context in machine-readable format. Mitigation: Establish interface contract early; define worksite context data model.
Risk: Performance target update frequency may exceed system capacity. Mitigation: Define update frequency requirements; design for scalability.
Risk: Traceability matrix may become difficult to maintain as system evolves. Mitigation: Implement automated traceability tracking; use configuration management tools.

Risk: Baseline versioning and change control procedures may not be followed in practice. Mitigation: Implement automated versioning in Performance Management subsystem; enforce change control via system design.
evidence_needed: Worksite context data model and Safety Services interface specification.
Performance derivation algorithm specification (rule set, decision tree, or optimization model) with examples.
Telemetry interface schema for performance targets (JSON, protobuf, or other).
Baseline versioning and change control procedures.
Traceability matrix template and maintenance procedures.
Stakeholder review and approval of performance target derivation approach.
Test cases and acceptance criteria for each sub-FR.
Performance Management subsystem architecture and interface design.
